items.headline: PLD Athena Phase B site work permitted at Rue Ferrari
items.summary: A $35 million commercial project receives its building permit for site work. This is the largest single project in this permit batch and suggests significant development activity in that area.

items.headline: Bank of America office tenant improvement permitted on Winchester
items.summary: Bank of America receives a $2.4 million permit for office buildout at 560 S Winchester Boulevard, indicating the bank is expanding or relocating its local operations.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument artwork agreement approved for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo, to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will be a permanent installation in the park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District sets FY 2026-27 budget
items.summary: City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 budget report and annual assessment, setting the financial plan for the district's operations and services.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of the General Plan, which guides development and policy through 2040. The General Plan shapes zoning, infrastructure, and land-use decisions across Cupertino.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This follows a period without a permanent appointment in that role.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water shortage plans and urban water strategy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed drafts of the 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents set the city's long-term water supply strategy and response measures if supplies tighten.

items.headline: Spring grants announced for community events and neighborhood projects
items.summary: The city considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications for the spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can apply for funding to run local events and neighborhood improvement projects.
